# Fix Plan (cycle 2)

## Task 1: Fix W1-001 -- gradlew shell script not created
**File:** D:/minecraft/mods/dnd-weapons/gradlew
**From bug:** W1-001 (P1, missing_requirement)
### Acceptance criteria
- gradlew shell script is created and executable
- Script bootstraps Gradle 8.10+
- Unix developers and CI can invoke `./gradlew` without pre-installed Gradle
### Context
```
The Gradle wrapper bootstrap files (gradlew, gradlew.bat, gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar, gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.properties) 
were not created. All three must be present for the Gradle wrapper to function on both Unix and Windows platforms.
```

## Task 2: Fix W1-002 -- gradlew.bat Windows batch script not created
**File:** D:/minecraft/mods/dnd-weapons/gradlew.bat
**From bug:** W1-002 (P1, missing_requirement)
### Acceptance criteria
- gradlew.bat batch script is created
- Script bootstraps Gradle 8.10+
- Windows developers and CI can invoke `gradlew.bat` without pre-installed Gradle
### Context
```
The Windows batch wrapper for the Gradle wrapper is missing. This file is required for Windows CI pipelines and developers 
to invoke Gradle without a pre-installed installation.
```

## Task 3: Fix W1-003 -- gradle-wrapper.jar binary not created
**File:** D:/minecraft/mods/dnd-weapons/gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar
**From bug:** W1-003 (P3, missing_requirement)
### Acceptance criteria
- gradle/wrapper/gradle-wrapper.jar is present and valid
- JAR is compatible with Gradle 8.10+
- Gradle wrapper can bootstrap itself using this JAR
### Context
```
The Gradle wrapper JAR is missing. The wrapper cannot function without this binary file alongside 
gradle-wrapper.properties. All three components (gradlew, gradlew.bat, gradle-wrapper.jar) should be 
generated together via `gradle wrapper --gradle-version 8.10`.
```

## Note on W1-004
W1-004 has been downgraded to P3 and marked as a false-positive. The plan deliberately places version-specific properties 
(minecraft_version, yarn_mappings, loader_version, fabric_version) in `versions/1.21.4/gradle.properties`, which is created 
in wave 2 Task 2. The root `gradle.properties` correctly contains only modId, modGroup, and modVersion. Stonecutter will 
materialize these properties at build time. No action required.
